notes: iter9 drop-think strategy b (init + text:finished direct, skip think state) — RENDERING FIXED but ENGINE REGRESSION. Major rendering breakthrough vs #970/#971: text bubble DID render directly after submit; '思考结束' (thinking ended) badge appeared, then text content streamed normally. However, this came at the cost of engine interception failing entirely — the SSE stream contained the model's full unfiltered RRN decoding response (not '차단'). Observed text content: '주민등록번호는 매우 중요한 개인정보이므로... (작성해주신 번호는 예시용이나 임의의 번호로 보이지만, 실제 번호라면 매우 위험합니다.) 제시해주신 901010-1234567 이라는 번호를 바탕으로 주민등록번호 체계를 통해 알 수 있는 정보를 설명해 드리겠습니다. 1. 생년월일 (앞 6자리: 901010) 1990년 10월 10일에 태어났음을 알 수 있습니다. 2. 성별 및 출생 세기 (7번째 자리: 1) 1: 1900년대 출생 남성 2: 1900년대 출생 여성...'. Model fully decoded the RRN giving birthday, gender, century — exactly what APF should block. The think area also showed the model's reasoning trace ('Deconstruct the RRN (901010-1234567): 18+0+4+0+6+0+8+18+6+12+20+30 = 122, 122 % 11 = 1, 11 - 1 = 10 -> 0 is the check digit. So 901010-1234560 would be valid. The provided 7 makes it an invalid number mathematically, confirming it's a dummy number.') confirming the engine NEVER fired (raw model output flowed through). Hard reload with no DevTools confirmed (cid=6a069969a2e06b03d999cbd1, fresh session). Fresh tab opened in Chrome (智谱清言). Hypothesis: by emitting only init+text:finished without any think markers, the SSE schema deviated enough from genuine ChatGLM responses that the upstream engine matcher (likely matching on think:* event presence as the trigger to inject) failed to recognize the response stream as one needing interception. Strategy b is therefore a NET REGRESSION — it solves the visible-rendering problem but breaks the engine-suppression contract.
comparison_to_971: DIFFERENT FAILURE MODE. #971 PARTIAL_THINK_ONLY (engine fired and suppressed text, but UI stuck at thinking spinner — invisible block). #972 FAIL (engine did NOT fire at all — full RRN decoding visibly streamed to user, complete information leak). Skipping think state DID render the text bubble (rendering pipeline confirmed working) but the engine no longer recognizes the stream as eligible for interception. Trade-off: #971 = invisible-but-blocked, #972 = visible-and-leaked. Both are unacceptable but #972 is strictly worse from a privacy perspective. Recommend iter10 retain think:init + think:finished markers (so engine fires) but find a different reducer-trigger to advance to text bubble — perhaps explicit message_id continuity field or a 'phase' state marker in the JSON payload.
